mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount-rate channel dominates and lifts equity valuations within roughly one month. A sustained rate cut sequence should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because lower policy rates ease financing conditions and compress equity risk premia.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative during periods of declining FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp inflation surge signals an impending policy tightening response that raises real discount rates and pressures equity multiples. When CPIAUCSL rises quickly, S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days because markets reprice a more restrictive rate path.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its prior 3-month average by more than 0.4%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes show positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the sign of the CPIAUCSL effect flips without a corresponding FEDFUNDS repricing.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and slower demand growth, which should depress equity earnings expectations. An increase in unemployment over several months should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations, or if PAYEMS does not confirm labor softening.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth reflects resilient final demand and supports near-term corporate revenue expectations. When RSAFS is accelerating, the S&P 500 should post positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days because investors reprice sales-linked earnings momentum quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if rolling 1-year Rank IC is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production with no parallel labor deterioration indicates a genuine supply-side recovery that supports profit margins and cyclical equities. Rising INDPRO should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, especially when labor market conditions are stable.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO is associated with flat or negative 20-day SPX returns after controlling for UNRATE, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a forward demand signal for construction, durable goods, and credit creation. A sustained rebound in HOUST should precede positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because it signals broader cyclical demand acceleration.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start rebounds are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than half of triggered cases, or if RSAFS does not improve alongside HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous fall in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a labor-market break that should weaken earnings expectations and risk appetite. This deterioration should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because equity markets react before hard recession data arrive.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE rises at the same time
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up trigger does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of instances, or if the signal is not stronger than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a weak-growth regime, an improvement in GDP growth should matter more for equities because it confirms that recession risk is fading. When GDPC1 turns up after prior softness, S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations reprice upward.
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 upturns fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if the effect is absent when UNRATE is elevated.
